title: Gravity, but sideways
intro: Welcome to the particle chamber. The blue haze is a uniform electric field filling the box — to an electron, it acts exactly like gravity acts on a thrown ball, just pointed by the field and scaled by charge-to-mass. Pull back the launcher and hit the target. You already know this physics: it is a projectile problem.
explain: F = qE plays the role of F = mg: constant force, so the electron flies a parabola — identical kinematics to a thrown ball, with a = qE/m ≈ 3.5×10¹³ m/s² here. That absurd number is the point: an electron's charge-to-mass ratio (1.76×10¹¹ C/kg) makes fields the strongest handle on matter we have. Every CRT, oscilloscope, electron microscope, and ion implanter is this level with better engineering.

prediction.prompt: Sketch the path a straight, full-speed launch will take.
